evaluations.question: Which Star Wars-related locations would Tim enjoy during his visit to Ireland?

top_50.generated_answer:
- Skellig Michael (County Kerry) – The island that portrays Ahch‑To in The Force Awakens and The Last Jedi. Boat landings run seasonally (typically May–Sept) from Portmagee/Valentia; seas can cancel trips, so plan flexibly.
- Ceann Sibeal/Sybil Head, Dingle Peninsula (County Kerry) – Hilltop where they built the beehive‑hut set for Ahch‑To in The Last Jedi. Stunning coastal views; reachable by car/hike on the peninsula.
- Dunmore Head, Dingle Peninsula (County Kerry) – Another Ahch‑To coastline spot used for Rey/Luke sequences. Short coastal walks with dramatic cliffs.
- Malin Head, Inishowen Peninsula (County Donegal) – Filming site for The Last Jedi (Millennium Falcon coastline shots). Wild, rocky scenery at Ireland’s northernmost point (“Bamba’s Crown”).
- Brow Head, near Crookhaven (County Cork) – Featured in The Last Jedi for additional Ahch‑To coastline shots. Panoramic ocean views from the headland.
- The Skellig Experience Visitor Centre, Valentia Island (County Kerry) – If seas are rough or boats are booked, this museum offers exhibits on Skellig Michael’s history and its Star Wars connection, plus great coastal viewpoints.

From Galway, the Dingle Peninsula and Valentia/Portmagee are popular weekend-road‑trip targets; Malin Head (north) and Brow Head (far southwest) are longer drives but epic for committed Star Wars fans.
